schema.name: Taypro Large-Scale Solar Panel Cleaning
schema.description: Robotic solar panel cleaning for 50–500 MW utility plants in India — fleet deployment, commissioning, and managed OPEX or CAPEX programmes.

hero.title: Large-scale solar panel cleaning — MW-scale robotic fleet deployment
hero.subtitle: 100 MW is not a pilot — it is a production O&M challenge. Taypro deploys multi-robot fleets on SECI, DVC, and IPP projects from 50 MW to 360 MW single sites. For pan-India service coverage proof, see the national fleet coverage page; for OPEX commercial terms, use the primary cleaning service page.

card0.label: Largest single-site automatic deployment

card0.title: Proven multi-MW references

card1.body: Engineering models for robots-per-MW, night-window coverage, and cycle count to hit PR recovery targets.

card2.body: Regional breakdown response, spare parts inventory, and remote diagnostics for fleets operating in remote solar parks.

card4.body: Zone-by-zone robot deployment on 200 MW+ sites minimises operational disruption. Commissioning playbook includes mapping, connectivity, operator training, and parallel O&M coordination.

card5.title: SECI and CPSU compliance alignment
card5.body: Cleaning SOPs, NECTYR logs, and monthly reconciliation structured for utility tender compliance and lender technical due diligence.

prose.heading: How many cleaning robots per MW at large scale?
prose.p1: There is no universal robots-per-MW ratio — it depends on fixed tilt vs tracker, row length, transfer points, target cycles per month, and battery swap strategy. Taypro engineers model fleet size during site assessment using stored row geometry and throughput targets.
prose.p2: On 100 MW+ tracker plants, GLYDE-X or NYUMA-X fleets with CRADYL row transfer are standard. Fixed-tilt blocks at 200–360 MW may deploy tens of GLYDE units with coordinated NECTYR scheduling across zones.
prose.p3: Browse Taypro's automatic project references or request a fleet model for your specific plant layout.

stateGrid.heading: Large-scale cleaning by state
stateGrid.intro: India's largest utility solar parks sit in Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Madhya Pradesh. Explore state-specific robotic cleaning guides.

faq.heading: Large-scale solar cleaning — FAQ
faq.subheading: Enterprise procurement questions for 50–500 MW plants.
faq.q0: Can Taypro clean a 100 MW solar plant?
faq.a0: Yes. Taypro has multiple 100 MW+ references and single-site deployments up to 360 MW with automatic GLYDE fleets. Fleet size and mobilisation timeline are modelled per site.
faq.q1: How many robots are needed per MW?
faq.a1: Robots-per-MW varies by installation type, row geometry, and cycle count. Taypro provides a site-specific fleet model during engineering assessment — not a generic brochure number.
faq.q2: What is the commissioning timeline for a 200 MW fleet?
faq.a2: Phased mobilisation is typical: site mapping, zone-by-zone robot deployment, operator training, and NECTYR onboarding. Timeline depends on row count and access — proposed during commercial engagement.
faq.q3: Does Taypro serve SECI and CPSU projects?
faq.a3: Yes. Taypro has experience on SECI-tendered and state discom utility plants with robotic cleaning fleets and managed OPEX programmes.
faq.q4: CAPEX or OPEX for 100 MW+ plants?
faq.a4: Both models are used at scale. CAPEX suits long hold periods and balance-sheet capacity. OPEX suits fast mobilisation and predictable per-panel opex. Many IPPs run portfolio-level analysis using our ROI calculator.
faq.q5: How does large-scale cleaning differ from utility operations context?
faq.a5: This page focuses on MW-scale fleet deployment capability and references. Our utility operations page covers industry context — soiling, PR, and O&M gaps. Our O&M services page covers procurement scope and SLAs.
faq.q6: What robots are used at MW scale?
faq.a6: GLYDE for fixed tilt, GLYDE-X and NYUMA-X for trackers, with CRADYL row transfer on long tracker tables. HELYX for scattered blocks within large sites.
faq.q7: How do I start an enterprise inquiry?
faq.a7: Share plant MW, location, installation type, SECI/IPP context, target go-live, and preferred commercial model. Taypro will assign engineering and propose fleet plan and pricing.
faq.q8: What is the largest single-site deployment Taypro has cleaned?
faq.a8: 360 MW automatic GLYDE deployment in Rajasthan (Akhadana reference). Contact Taypro for current fleet references matching your MW scale.
faq.q9: How long to mobilise a 200 MW cleaning fleet?
faq.a9: Phased mobilisation over weeks depending on zone count, access, and row geometry. Detailed schedule proposed during commercial engagement.
faq.q10: Can Taypro clean multiple blocks with different installation types on one site?
faq.a10: Yes. Mixed fixed-tilt and tracker blocks within large solar parks are common. Fleet model assigns GLYDE, GLYDE-X, or NYUMA-X per block with unified NECTYR oversight.
faq.q11: What documentation do lenders request for robotic cleaning programmes?
faq.a11: NECTYR cycle logs, coverage maps, monthly panel-volume reconciliation, and cleaning SOPs. Taypro programmes are structured for technical due diligence on utility solar finance.

steps.heading: MW-scale solar cleaning robot mobilisation process
steps.step0Body: Share single-site MW or multi-site portfolio, installation mix, SECI/IPP context, and commercial model preference (CAPEX vs OPEX).
steps.step1Body: Robots-per-MW calculated from row geometry, tracker vs fixed tilt, cycle targets, and CRADYL placement. Not generic industry ratios.
steps.step2Body: Pricing, breakdown response, reporting cadence, and escalation matrix scaled to plant size. OPEX or CAPEX terms issued.
steps.step3Body: Zone-by-zone mobilisation on large sites. Operators, spares, and NECTYR onboarding per phase.
steps.step4Body: 3–10 cycles monthly with monthly panel-volume reconciliation and quarterly programme reviews.

eligibility.title: Large-scale solar panel cleaning — when Taypro fits
eligibility.for0: Utility solar plants 50 MW to 500 MW single site or portfolio
eligibility.for1: SECI, state discom, CPSU, and IPP assets with documented MW references
eligibility.for2: Fixed-tilt and single-axis tracker plants requiring multi-robot fleets
eligibility.for3: Procurement teams needing enterprise SLA, NECTYR audit trail, and pan-India service
eligibility.not0: Sub-10 MW ground-mount without feasibility exception
eligibility.not1: Residential or small C&I rooftop (see rooftop guide)
eligibility.not2: Plants seeking one annual manual wash only

highlight.heading: 100 MW is not a pilot.
highlight.lead: Large-scale solar panel cleaning requires production fleet operations — not a handful of demo robots.
highlight.body: Taypro deploys tens of robots on 200–360 MW sites with NECTYR fleet oversight, regional spares, and dedicated operators. That is the bar for MW-scale cleaning procurement in India.

proseSecondary.heading: 100MW solar cleaning and beyond — Taypro deployment evidence
proseSecondary.p1: Akhadana Rajasthan 360 MW, Bachau DVC Gujarat 300 MW, and Neneva Gujarat 250 MW represent automatic-first Taypro GLYDE fleets at the upper end of Indian utility scale. These are production deployments — not demonstration arrays.
proseSecondary.p2: Fleet sizing on 100 MW tracker plants typically involves GLYDE-X or NYUMA-X with CRADYL transfer stations at row ends. Fixed-tilt 200 MW blocks may run coordinated multi-zone GLYDE fleets with NECTYR scheduling across zones.
proseSecondary.p3: Request enterprise proposal with plant layout, or browse automatic project case studies filtered by MW capacity.
